reviews.review_description: On the surface, it seems simple enough, but there is so much to this game and it's going to take a while to get to it all. The graphics are really nice and the game is really polished. The puzzle strategy cycle is addictive. The most surprising thing is I didn't hear about this when it released 10 months ago.

Was holding off on getting this game for months, but it so fun and addictive. 

The storylines and upgrades there’s just so much to do great puzzle game. 

Feels like you’re inside a network actively getting data must buy 100%

I always loved DarkNet, ever since it came out on the original Oculus Rift. It's been effectively adapted to feel natural and look beautiful on every platform since then. It's a simple action-puzzle game sort of like Tetris, but its storytelling and theming (which is all environmental and subtle) really makes your feel like a dark net hacker, slowly penetrating into secure systems, changing the world just by retrieving data for money. It's a really comfortable seated VR game with little to no artificial locomotion (the bits it has are optional), so it should be safe for everyone. Highly recommended indie VR jewel.
Ive happily bought this game twice now.  The gameplay loop of solving a bunch of little puzzles in order to solve a big puzzle might be my favorite gameplay loop, and this has that in spades.

For a long time, I have been waiting for not only a game based around the dark web to come out in VR, but one that takes place in a cyberspace that's literally the internet. This is it.

I still can't believe that this game came out 12 years ago, although the game is simplistic enough for it to justify being a bare bones type of experience... ON THE SURFACE.

First off, I love how even though you play the role of hacking a network, it still feels immersive because you feel like you're inside a giant web that is the internet.  I also love how I can tell what everything is easily, such as the viruses being purple, the antiviruses being baby blue, and the core being yellow, oh and let's not forget about the green crystals that represent a massive amount of money.

Since a lot of you already know what this game is like since it's near retro at this point, let me get right into my opinion... The puzzles are simple yet frustrating.  They're also not really predictable either, which would be massively frustrating if you're trying to Speedrun these networks.  I will say I really don't like how you could completely get rid of the firewalls from a node and you will still have to deal with 3 green antiviruses ANYWAY. 

The way the networks are presented in the network select screen also look like little planets or stars in the solar system that you need to fly towards, which is beautiful but eerie at the same time.

Some of the networks have multiple webs you can travel towards within a network, and when you click on the wormhole to find these other webs, you are greeted with going through a tunnel and then going straight into a massive planet like structure that also has a bunch of other planets surrounding it, which again I'd super eerie and dramatic.

As I am writing this, I have JUST unlocked the Backbone Access network, which in simplistic terms is the final boss of the game.  My god when I spent all 30,000 of my Bitcoin that I have been grinding FOR DAYS JUST PLAYING THIS GAME, I was greeted with a massive network that was honestly so terrifying to look at! I honestly felt like I was falling into Jupiter's surface it was that massive.  Not to mention, the fact that the root node is protected by a big green crystal because literally all of the nodes inside the one web are protected by blue crystals... It's all super eerie and uncomfortable, but in a good way that makes me feel like I taking on the literal final boss of the internet. I'm legit surprised the developer didn't name it The Primarch System instead of just Backbone Access.

Was so excited to hear after sending an email a month or so before it came out asking about it that this was coming! Spent wayy too many hours playing it via PC for the last 10 years and finally can do it without wires or streaming woo! 

Thank you so much for porting this legend!! If you're on the fence, don't be! Game is fantastic and has huge replayability!

It’s very difficult to nail the core synergy of these two genres, but Darknet’s “do I need to power up more and come back to this one or do I just need to think harder?” dilemma is so tightly devised that this game will still be great in 2125. Glad to see it back: it looks great, the way Jon St. John says “visualization” still cracks me up,  and being able to rotate around in my office chair without strangling myself on a cord definitely adds to the immersion.

reviews.review_title: An addicting puzzle within a puzzle
reviews.review_description:
You can watch my video review here.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6EMKB5_M_SE

Addictive Gameplay: Easily lose hours without realizing it.  

Simple Controls: Laser pointer aiming and looking around. Not a huge reason for this to be a VR game, but I still found it immersive.

Puzzle Roguelike: Procedurally generated networks. Hacking is a puzzle within a puzzle.
Strategic Hacking: Use viruses, worms, exploits, and hydras to take over nodes and reach the root. Timed challenges add pressure.

Multiple Tools: Viruses, hydras, exploits, and worms offer varied hacking strategies.
Upgrades: Earn bitcoin to upgrade starting cash, access higher difficulties, or get faster viruses. No game-breaking perks.

High Replay Value: Procedural generation and layered difficulty ensure lots to explore.
Affordable: Only $10.

VR Mobile Game Feel: A good mobile game experience translated to VR. Great for short bursts or long sessions.

Hidden Gem: Feels like a must-try for puzzle and strategy fans.
Surely one of the best games available for the VR. Smart, entertaining, and clearly designed for VR specifically.

I played Darknet back in the early Rift days, and its predecessor Ciess, on GearVR before that.  It turns out it's every bit as strong of a game now as it was then, with the added bonus of an improved tutorial and graphical upgrades to its gorgeous neon-infused geometry.  This is hacking as William Gibson envisioned it in Neuromancer, and the game demands careful strategy executed under increasingly tense time pressure.  It's not a huge game, but the core mechanics are solid, and it can be incredible addictive to play.  I've logged a decent number of hours on this new version, and I'm looking forward to putting in plenty more.  Highly recommended.

Being an established fan of E McNeill’s games especially IRONSTRIKE made me eager to play Dark Net which successfully fulfilled my expectations. McNeill consistently produces polished and thoughtful experiences which engage players deeply and Dark Net continues this trend. McNeill demonstrates his ability to master multiple genres while preserving his unique design philosophy through Dark Net which captures IRONSTRIKE’s strategic depth and immersive qualities but takes players to a completely new setting. 

The true highlight is the flawless and refined quality of the overall experience. Dark Net maintains McNeill’s tradition of delivering games with refined and intuitive gameplay loops. The basic game mechanics are easy to understand yet present complex layers that maintain your interest for extended periods. The game’s expertly designed atmosphere captivates you with its interactive high-tech universe that feels dynamic and responsive. 

The dedication developers show when polishing their games earns my support and McNeill’s latest project is an example of this excellence. Dark Net demonstrates McNeill's exceptional attention to detail and dedication to quality which he previously established in IRONSTRIKE. I admire developers who maintain high standards in VR creation and eagerly anticipate their upcoming projects. Dark Net delivers essential strategic gameplay and immersion for any enthusiast of immersive experiences.
Was so disappointed it wasn't available on quest until this remaster! A great game that deserves a bigger audience!

reviews.review_title: As others say, a dream come true!
reviews.review_description:
I really loved playing Darknet in the past and was really sad it wasn’t available on Quest 2 / 3. 

Instant purchase for me (and loving it on the Quest 3) ! 

Happy times indeed! ❤️
Had almost given up hope of ever seeing a Quest port of this. Nice surprise today. Love it.
Great puzzle game with great polish. Highly recommend.
The base. One of the VR games that made VR history. Happy to see this official port.
